question: Define a `struct Book` with members `title` (`std::string`), `author` (`std::string`), and `publicationYear` (int). Write a program that declares an array of three `Book` structs, prompts the user to enter details for each book, and then prints the details of all books.

test_cases.input:
The Great Gatsby
F. Scott Fitzgerald
1925
Book Two Title
Author Two
2001
Book Three Title
Author Three
2002
test_cases.expected:
Enter details for three books:

--- Book 1 ---
Enter title for book 1: Enter author for book 1: Enter publication year for book 1: 
--- Book 2 ---
Enter title for book 2: Enter author for book 2: Enter publication year for book 2: 
--- Book 3 ---
Enter title for book 3: Enter author for book 3: Enter publication year for book 3: 

--- Details of all books ---

Book 1:
Title: The Great Gatsby
Author: F. Scott Fitzgerald
Publication Year: 1925

Book 2:
Title: 
Author: Book Two Title
Publication Year: 2001

Book 3:
Title: 
Author: Book Three Title
Publication Year: 2002

errors: The program does not properly handle leftover newline characters in the input buffer after reading an integer (`std::cin >> int`). This causes `std::getline` to read an empty string for the subsequent `title` input in the next iteration, leading to incorrect or missing data for books after the first one.

The program lacks input validation for `publicationYear`. If non-numeric input is provided, `std::cin` enters a fail state, and subsequent input operations will fail or misbehave, potentially leading to crashes or incorrect data without proper error handling (e.g., clearing the error state and ignoring bad input).
